channel.title: PodLens
channel.description: Grounded readings, every point traceable to the source.

item.title: The Era of Experience: Reinforcement Learning Beyond Human Data · David Silver
item.description: This paper is written by David Silver and Richard S. Sutton (the text is a preprint of a chapter in the forthcoming book Designing an Intelligence to be published by MIT Press). The paper explores how…

item.title: The Economics of AGI and Automation · Alex Imas & Phil Trammell
item.description: In this episode, host Dwarkesh Patel interviews Alex Imas (Director of AGI Economics at Google DeepMind and Professor of Economics at the University of Chicago) and Phil Trammell (Head of Economics at…

item.title: Representation Learning and Predictive World Models · Saining Xie
item.description: This episode features an in-depth, marathon interview with young scientist and entrepreneur Xie Saining, alongside a brief guest segment with Tommy (Zhiyuan Zeng). The central focus of the discussion …

item.title: The Reality of Frontier AI and the End of Individual Heroism · Yao Shunyu
item.description: In this episode, host Xiaojun interviews Yao Shunyu, a prominent AI researcher who transitioned from theoretical physics to AI, having previously worked at Anthropic and currently at Google DeepMind. …

item.title: How an AI Chip Works from the Bottom Up · Reiner Pope
item.description: In this episode, the host interviews Reiner Pope, CEO of the AI chip company MatX, to understand how an AI chip works from the bottom up. Starting with the most fundamental logic gates, Pope explains …

item.title: The Core Algorithm of AlphaGo · Eric Jang
item.description: Guest Eric Jang, a researcher who recently rebuilt a Go-playing AI, explains the core principles of AlphaGo. The episode walks through the rules of Go, the intractability of its game tree for classica…

item.title: The Unification of Physics · Don Lincoln
item.description: Host Lex Fridman speaks with Don Lincoln, a particle physicist at Fermilab, about the history and future of physics through the lens of unification. The central theme is the centuries-long quest to sh…

item.title: The Three Realms of the Universe · Hakeem Oluseyi
item.description: Astrophysicist Hakeem Oluseyi discusses three fundamental "realms" of the universe from his book, Why Do We Exist?. The talk follows a logical arc from the smallest scale to the largest and most specu…

item.title: Rampant Natural Selection in the Bronze Age · David Reich
item.description: The host interviews guest David Reich, a professor of ancient DNA at Harvard, about a new preprint from his lab. The central topic is the detection of natural selection in human populations in Europe …

item.title: The Four Problems of Time · Jim Al-Khalili
item.description: Physicist Jim Al-Khalili discusses the physics of time, outlining what he calls the four distinct "problems of time": whether time flows, how to reconcile quantum field theory with general relativity,…
